Global (All).title: Riots in Iran amid growing tensions with Israel
Global (All).summary: Riots have broken out in Iran's capital city of Tehran, amid growing tensions with Israel. The protests are led by opposition groups who are calling for the government to intervene in the escalating standoff with Israel.

Global (All).title: US-China trade tensions escalate
Global (All).summary: The US and China have announced new trade measures, escalating tensions between the two countries. The US has imposed new tariffs on Chinese goods, and China has retaliated with its own set of tariffs.

Global (All).title: Cyber attack hits major US hospital
Global (All).summary: A major US hospital has been hit by a cyber attack, which has resulted in the theft of sensitive patient data. The attack is believed to have been carried out by a sophisticated hacking group.

United States.summary: Mass shootings have increased over the past week in the United States, sparking renewed debate over gun control.
The US Supreme Court's recent decision to limit Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) authority has raised concerns about environmental policy and its impact on public health.
A recent survey suggests a slight rise in anti-vaccination sentiment among Americans, potentially threatening public health initiatives.

South Korea.title: N. Korea fires projectiles in suspected missile tests
South Korea.summary: South Korea's military says North Korea fired projectiles into its eastern waters, with the exact type and range still unclear, amid a recent flurry of missile tests. The South's Joint Chiefs of Staff reported the launches, describing them as 'suspected missile tests.'

South Korea.title: US imposes sanctions on Chinese companies for North Korea ties
South Korea.summary: The US Treasury has imposed sanctions on several Chinese companies and individuals for allegedly doing business with North Korea in defiance of international restrictions. The US sees China as key to pressuring North Korea to give up its nuclear arsenal.

South Korea.title: Japan, South Korea to discuss disputed isles
South Korea.summary: A Japanese defense official says Tokyo and Seoul will have a meeting soon to discuss their long-standing dispute over a group of islets in the Korea Strait. The dispute has strained ties between the US allies.

Japan.title: Tokyo reports first COVID-19 cases in over two weeks
Japan.summary: Tokyo reported 23 new COVID-19 cases on Sunday, its first cases in over two weeks, as Japan sees a resurgence in virus cases.

Japan.title: Japan's ruling party to propose constitutional revision bills
Japan.summary: Japan's ruling Liberal Democratic Party will propose bills to revise the country's post-war pacifist constitution, a long-held goal, on Friday.

Japan.title: Typhoon Hinnamor makes landfall in Japan
Japan.summary: Typhoon Hinnamor made landfall in Japan on Wednesday, bringing strong winds and heavy rain, with the country's weather agency issuing a rare warning for a typhoon's 'violent windstorms'.
